key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = catégorie spécifique depuis 2018; enregistrement NFSA requis avant importation/vente; dossier: composition; sécurité; fabricant; LMS; étiquetage en arabe; mentions obligatoires; interdiction claims médicaux; GMP exigé
notes: NFSA créée 2017; régime compléments en construction; processus enregistrement 6-12 mois; importation: certificat d'origine notarisé + analyse lot; agent local requis

regulation_ref: Loi n° 28-07 relative à la sécurité sanitaire des produits alimentaires + Arrêté spécifique compléments
enforcement_body: ONSSA (Office National de Sécurité Sanitaire des Produits Alimentaires)
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = catégorie alimentaire; notification/enregistrement ONSSA requis; liste substances actives; LMS; étiquetage en français et/ou arabe; mentions obligatoires DJR; interdiction claims thérapeutiques; certificat de libre vente pays d'origine requis pour importation
notes: Maroc: régime inspiré du modèle EU; ONSSA moins strict que EFSA mais en convergence progressive; arrêté spécifique compléments en cours de révision (2024-2025)

regulation_name: Complementary Medicines SAHPRA — Afrique du Sud
regulation_ref: Foodstuffs Cosmetics and Disinfectants Act No. 54 of 1972 + Complementary Medicines Regulations (SAHPRA 2022)
enforcement_body: SAHPRA (South African Health Products Regulatory Authority)
key_requirements: Compléments vitamines/minéraux = «Complementary Medicine» sous SAHPRA (pas food); enregistrement SAHPRA obligatoire; dossier: formule; GMP; efficacité; sécurité; étiquetage en anglais minimum; LMS selon Annexe SAHPRA; validité enregistrement 5 ans; GMP SA required
notes: ZA = régime pharma-light pour compléments; SAHPRA a rationalisé en 2022; transition des anciens produits en cours jusqu'à 2026; importation = agent local SAHPRA + MCC registration

enforcement_body: NAFDAC (National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control)
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = catégorie NAFDAC; enregistrement obligatoire avant commercialisation (NAFDAC number); dossier: composition; fabricant; GMP certificate; sécurité; étiquetage en anglais; liste substances autorisées; LMS; interdiction claims thérapeutiques; validité 5 ans
notes: NAFDAC enregistrement: 6-18 mois; coût USD variable; importation nécessite agent nigérian agréé; contrefaçons = enjeu majeur; NAFDAC effectue raids marchés; renouvellement automatique si conformité

regulation_name: KEBS + Pharmacy and Poisons Board Kenya
regulation_ref: Kenya Bureau of Standards (KEBS) + Pharmacy and Poisons Board (PPB) + Food Drugs and Chemical Substances Act
key_requirements: Compléments vitamines = zone grise Kenya; PPB régule si claim thérapeutique (registration obligatoire); KEBS régule comme food si no claim; pas de cadre dédié compléments alimentaires; étiquetage en anglais; importation: certificat d'origine + analyse; agent local
notes: Kenya: cadre réglementaire incomplet pour compléments; PPB a tendance à saisir produits sans registration; KEBS + PPB en discussion pour créer régime unifié; risque de saisie douanière élevé

regulation_ref: CAC/GL 55-2005 (Guidelines for Vitamin and Mineral Food Supplements)
key_requirements: Lignes directrices non contraignantes pour compléments vitamines/minéraux; définit: champ application; liste positive substances; LMS basées sur évaluation risques; étiquetage; allégations; GMP; sert de référence aux pays sans réglementation propre + base pour harmonisation WTO/SPS
notes: CAC/GL 55-2005 = référence internationale clé; pas de force obligatoire mais utilisé dans litiges WTO; nombreux pays en développement s'alignent sur Codex faute de réglementation nationale propre

regulation_name: ISO 22000 — Système de management sécurité alimentaire
key_requirements: Norme de système de management; applicable aux fabricants de compléments alimentaires; certif. ISO 22000 reconnue par de nombreuses autorités; combine HACCP + prérequis ISO; audit tierce-partie; souvent exigée par acheteurs B2B et par certaines autorités (ex. SFDA Saudi)
notes: ISO 22000 non obligatoire légalement mais devient quasi-exigence commerciale dans les appels d'offres; FSSC 22000 = version complète incluant ISO 22000 + prérequis sectoriels; reconnue GFSI

regulation_name: WADA Prohibited List — Risque anti-dopage compléments
regulation_ref: World Anti-Doping Code 2024 — Prohibited List International Standard (WADA)
key_requirements: WADA ne régule pas directement les compléments alimentaires; mais contaminants/adultérants dans compléments peuvent contenir substances WADA-prohibées (ex. stéroides; SARMs; stimulants); risque pour sportifs: responsabilité stricte WADA; INFORMED Sport / NSF Certified for Sport = certifications tierces recommandées pour athlètes
notes: WADA = risque secondaire pour fabricant; si complément vitamines contient inadvertamment stimulants = athlète sanctionné; claims «pour sportifs» augmentent risque réputationnel; certification NSF/INFORMED recommandée si marché sport

regulation_name: UNECE WP.29 — Non applicable aux compléments alimentaires
key_requirements: UNECE WP.29 régit les véhicules et équipements automobiles; aucune pertinence pour les compléments alimentaires vitamines/minéraux; juridiction incluse pour exhaustivité de la couverture
notes: Non applicable: UNECE WP.29 couvre réglementation automobile; sans lien avec compléments alimentaires

regulation_name: OCDE — Non applicable aux compléments alimentaires
key_requirements: L'OCDE émet des lignes directrices sur tests de sécurité chimiques (OECD Test Guidelines) et sur la conduite d'essais — applicables indirectement aux études toxicologiques; cependant pas de régulation directe OCDE sur compléments alimentaires; juridiction incluse pour exhaustivité
notes: Non applicable directement: OECD Test Guidelines peuvent être référencées dans dossiers toxicologiques mais OCDE ne régule pas les compléments alimentaires en tant que produit

regulation_name: EU Textile Fibre Names & Labelling Regulation
key_requirements: Mandatory indication of fibre composition on label (% by mass); use of harmonised fibre names (Annex I); country of origin optional but if stated must be accurate; care labelling symbols per ISO 3758; language of country of sale
notes: Directly applicable in all EU member states; synthetic fibres (polyester/polyamide/elastane) must use EU-harmonised names; tolerance +/-3% per fibre

regulation_ref: Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 - Annex XVII Entry 43 (azodyes) Entry 72 (CMR)
key_requirements: Restriction on azocolourants releasing carcinogenic aromatic amines (>30 mg/kg); restrictions on CMR substances in articles; SVHC candidate list substances >0.1% w/w in articles require notification/communication down supply chain; annual ECHA SVHC list updates
notes: Annex XVII Entry 43 applies to textile articles in direct prolonged skin contact; SVHC list exceeds 240 substances as of 2025; PFAS under SVHC review; synthetic sport textile may use PFAS finishes (DWR = durable water repellency)

regulation_ref: REACH Annex XVII - PFAS restriction dossier (expected adoption 2027)
key_requirements: Universal restriction on manufacture/use/placing on market of PFAS substances; derogations under discussion for essential uses; transition periods 18 months-12 years depending on sector; DWR finishes on sportswear likely affected; fluoropolymer scope debated
notes: Dossier submitted 2023 by DE/DK/NL/NO/SE; SEAC/RAC opinions expected 2025-2026; adoption as Annex XVII entry expected 2027; legging sport with DWR/antimicrobial PFAS finishes directly impacted

regulation_name: ESPR - Eco-design + Digital Product Passport textile
key_requirements: Framework for product sustainability requirements; textile-specific delegated act to set: durability/repairability/recyclability requirements; mandatory Digital Product Passport (DPP) with fibre content/traceability/chemical info/recycled content; microplastics release limits for synthetic textiles
notes: Regulation adopted June 2024; textile delegated act consultation ongoing; DPP for textiles likely mandatory 2027+; synthetic legging with polyester/polyamide/elastane directly in scope; microplastics shedding from synthetic sportswear a key parameter

regulation_name: CSDDD - Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive
key_requirements: Mandatory human rights and environmental due diligence across value chain; identification/prevention/mitigation of adverse impacts; grievance mechanisms; climate transition plan aligned with Paris Agreement; phased scope: >5000 employees + >1.5bn turnover from 2027; >1000 employees from 2028
notes: Directive adopted July 2024; transposition deadline July 2026; garment/textile sector explicitly cited in Annex; covers cotton sourcing/dyeing/finishing supply chain; applies to EU companies and non-EU companies with EU turnover >1.5bn EUR

enforcement_body: EC DG FISMA + National financial regulators
key_requirements: Mandatory sustainability reporting per ESRS; double materiality assessment; ESRS E1 (climate) / E2 (pollution) / S1 (own workforce) / S2 (workers in value chain) especially relevant for textiles; phased from FY2024 (large listed companies) to FY2026+ (SMEs listed)
notes: ESRS S2 (workers in value chain) covers garment supply chain; ESRS E2 covers PFAS/chemical pollution; reporting on microplastics under discussion; applies to reporting entity not product but determines disclosures about product

regulation_name: EU Waste Framework Directive - Mandatory Textile EPR
regulation_ref: Directive (EU) 2018/851 as amended + Commission Delegated Regulation on textile EPR (2025)
enforcement_body: EC DG ENV + National EPR scheme operators
key_requirements: All EU member states must establish mandatory EPR schemes for textiles by end 2025; producers placing textile products on EU market must register and contribute to EPR scheme; eco-modulation of fees based on recyclability/durability; separate collection obligation from 1 Jan 2025
notes: Separate collection for textiles mandatory EU-wide from Jan 2025; national EPR scheme implementation varies by member state; FR Refashion is reference model; Delegated Regulation on EPR minimum requirements expected 2025

key_requirements: Prohibition on generic environmental claims without substantiation (eco, green, natural, sustainable, responsible); prohibition on carbon offset claims for neutrality; prohibition on sustainability labels not based on approved certification schemes; prohibition on early obsolescence tactics; applies from 27 March 2026
notes: Transposition deadline 27 March 2026; directly impacts marketing claims for sport textile; eco/sustainable/recycled/OEKO-TEX claims must be properly substantiated; amends Unfair Commercial Practices Directive

regulation_ref: COM(2023) 166 - Proposal for Directive on green claims
key_requirements: Mandatory pre-verification of explicit environmental claims before market placement; accredited verifier required; lifecycle assessment basis for claims; prohibited practices list; penalties up to 4% annual turnover; covers textiles
notes: Proposal published March 2023; legislative process ongoing; expected adoption 2025-2026; more stringent than Empowering Consumers Directive; both instruments likely to coexist

key_requirements: Interdiction de destruction des invendus textile (Art. 49); REP textile obligatoire; affichage environnemental obligatoire pour le textile (progressif); index reparabilite envisage; interdiction micro-billes plastiques; information sur presence de microplastiques synthetiques
notes: Decrets application multiples 2021-2023; affichage environnemental textile en experimentation via affichage.beta.gouv.fr; invendus textiles interdits de destruction depuis jan 2022

regulation_name: REP textile Refashion - Responsabilite Elargie du Producteur
regulation_ref: Art. L.541-10-3 Code environnement + Decret n. 2020-1725
key_requirements: Adhesion obligatoire a Refashion; declaration annuelle des tonnages mis sur marche; contribution financiere basee sur volume; eco-modulation selon criteres durabilite/recyclabilite (bonus/malus); contribution financement collecte separee et tri
notes: Refashion gere collecte + tri textile France; mise sur marche = obligation adhesion; legging synthetique = categorie vetements; eco-modulation selon presence PFAS et durabilite

key_requirements: Liste de 46 qualites environnementales pouvant etre affichees; conditions de justification pour chaque allegation; methodes de calcul validees; applicable aux textiles et produits de grande consommation; sanction si affichage non conforme
notes: Applicable depuis jan 2023; complementaire a la Directive Empowering Consumers EU; liste positive des claims autorises avec conditions; fibres recyclees/durabilite/biodegradabilite concernees

key_requirements: Plan de vigilance obligatoire pour societes >5000 salaries France ou >10000 monde; cartographie risques droits humains/environnementaux dans chaine approvisionnement; actions prevention/attenuation; mecanisme alerte; publication annuelle; secteur textile/habillement = secteur a risque eleve
notes: Pionniere mondiale (avant CSDDD); jurisprudence TotalEnergies/Yves Rocher en 2024 precise les obligations; chaine approvisionnement coton/fibres synthetiques visee

key_requirements: Obligations de diligence raisonnable droits humains et environnementaux; >3000 salaries depuis 2023; >1000 depuis 2024; analyse des risques annuelle; mesures preventives et correctives; mecanisme de plainte; rapport annuel public; secteur textile = secteur a risque prioritaire
notes: Plus strict que directive CSDDD sur certains points; BAFA publie lignes directrices sectorielles; co-applicable avec CSDDD apres transposition; chaine coton/fibres synthetiques (Asie) visee

key_requirements: Transposition nationale REACH; restrictions substances chimiques; Germany co-submitter REACH PFAS dossier (DE/DK/NL/NO/SE); national surveillance textile chemical content; GefStoffV (Hazardous Substances Ordinance) requirements
notes: Allemagne co-auteur du dossier restriction PFAS REACH; surveillance nationale PFAS dans textiles active (Umweltbundesamt); renforcement prevu post-adoption restriction REACH

key_requirements: Trasposizione nazionale Regolamento UE 1007/2011; etichettatura composizione fibra; denominazioni armonizzate; sanzioni per etichettatura non conforme; ispezioni doganali; lingua italiana obbligatoria per mercato italiano
notes: Conformita a Reg. UE 1007/2011 sufficiente; sanzioni nazionali fino a 25000 EUR per non conformita; MISE pubblica liste ispezioni prioritarie

regulation_ref: AGCM Linee Guida comunicazioni ambientali 2021 + aggiornamento 2023
enforcement_body: AGCM (Autorita Garante della Concorrenza e del Mercato)
key_requirements: Linee guida claims ambientali: specificita, veridicita, rilevanza, non-ingannevolezza; greenwashing = pratica commerciale scorretta; settore moda/abbigliamento under scrutiny; procedimenti AGCM vs brand sostenibilita 2022-2024; multa max 5mln EUR
notes: AGCM attiva su greenwashing moda: procedimenti Decathlon/H&M (2023-2024); claims eco o sostenibile su legging sintetico richiedono evidenze LCA

key_requirements: Transposicion nacional Reglamento UE 1007/2011; composicion fibras en etiqueta; denominaciones armonizadas; instrucciones conservacion (simbolos ISO 3758); idioma espanol obligatorio; regimen sancionador
notes: Real Decreto actualiza normativa espanola; inspeccion por CCAA; sanciones hasta 600.000 EUR para infracciones graves

regulation_name: Ley 7/2022 - Residuos y suelos contaminados (REP textile)
regulation_ref: Ley 7/2022 de 8 de abril de residuos y suelos contaminados
key_requirements: Regimen REP obligatorio textiles conforme directive UE 2018/851; registro productores; contribucion financiera gestion fin de vida; objetivo recogida separada; diseno ecocompatible; prohibicion destruccion excedentes (analoga AGEC FR)
notes: Implementa EPR textile EU en Espana; esquema nacional en curso de despliegue; legging sintetico = producto textil sometido REP

key_requirements: Due diligence obligations on child labour in supply chain for companies selling to Dutch consumers; investigation of supply chain for risk of child labour; action plan if risk identified; declaration to ACM; applies from 1 July 2023 (enforcement from 2024)
notes: NL child labour due diligence act; garment/textile sector = high risk sector; complement to CSDDD; supply chain from Bangladesh/India/Vietnam for synthetic fibre production in scope

regulation_name: Textile Products (Labelling and Fibre Composition) Regulations 2012
key_requirements: Fibre composition labelling by % mass; use of UK-specific fibre names (aligned with former EU list); care labelling (voluntary but must be accurate); country of origin not mandated; penalties for non-compliance; post-Brexit maintained with minor amendments
notes: Post-Brexit maintained as UK domestic law; largely mirrors former EU Reg 1007/2011; GB importers/retailers responsible for compliance; Trading Standards enforcement

regulation_ref: Retained Regulation (EC) 1907/2006 as amended by UK REACH (SI 2019/758)
key_requirements: Independent UK chemicals registration/notification system post-Brexit; UK REACH maintains former EU restrictions including Annex XVII azodyes restriction; SVHC list maintained separately (UK SHVC list); UK assessing PFAS restriction separately from EU
notes: UK PFAS restriction assessment ongoing (DEFRA/HSE); UK may diverge from EU on scope/timeline; synthetic textiles with DWR finishes must track both EU and UK PFAS restrictions

regulation_name: Modern Slavery Act 2015 - s.54 Supply Chain Transparency
key_requirements: Annual modern slavery and human trafficking statement required for commercial organisations with annual turnover >GBP 36m supplying goods/services in UK; statement must cover: supply chain structure; due diligence policies; risk areas; training; effectiveness KPIs; fashion/garment sector = high priority sector
notes: Garment sector heavily scrutinised; cotton from Xinjiang/Central Asia = high risk; 2024 review proposed mandatory content requirements and enforcement; links to UFLPA concerns

regulation_ref: SR 232.121 - Verordnung uber die Kennzeichnung von Textilprodukten
enforcement_body: SECO (Secretariat d Etat a l economie)
key_requirements: Etiquetage composition fibres textiles; designations des fibres alignees sur UE; instructions entretien (symboles ISO 3758); langue(s) officielle(s) suisse(s) selon region de vente; sanctions penales possibles; pas de notification prealable
notes: Suisse adapte ses regles aux standards UE via accords bilateraux; surveillance SECO; importateurs/distributeurs responsables

regulation_name: Tekstilfibrer - Forskrift om merking av tekstilprodukter
key_requirements: Merking av fibersammensetning (% vekt); bruk av harmoniserte fiberbenevnelser (EOS/EU-liste); vaskeanvisning etter ISO 3758; norsk tekst for norsk marked; EOS-avtalen innlemmer EU Reg. 1007/2011
notes: EEA member - integrates EU Reg 1007/2011 via EEA Agreement; Norway co-submitter REACH PFAS restriction dossier; Miljodirektoratet active on PFAS in textiles

regulation_name: EAEU TR TS 017/2011 - Produits de l industrie legere
regulation_ref: TR TS 017/2011 + Decision de la Commission de l Union douaniere N. 876
key_requirements: Exigences de securite pour produits textile/habillement; restrictions substances chimiques (formaldehyde, metaux lourds, colorants azoiques); marquage EAC obligatoire; declaration ou certification selon produit; composition fibres obligatoire; instructions entretien
notes: TR TS 017/2011 est un technical regulation de l Union Douaniere EAEU (RU/BY/KZ/AM/KG); marquage EAC = conformite verifiee; applicable pour exportation vers Russie et pays EAEU

regulation_name: EAEU TR TS 017/2011 - Technical Regulation Light Industry Products
regulation_ref: Decision No. 876 of the Customs Union Commission, November 9, 2011
key_requirements: Single technical regulation for light industry products across EAEU bloc; safety requirements: restricted substances (azo dyes, formaldehyde <75-300mg/kg by end use, pH 4.0-8.5, heavy metals); EAC marking mandatory; declaration of conformity or certification
notes: Covers all EAEU member states; legging sport = adult apparel category; formaldehyde limits vary by end-use (next to skin vs not); sampling and lab testing required for certification

regulation_ref: Tekstil Urunlerinin Adlandirilmasi Hakkinda Yonetmelik (2012/2 No lu Teblig)
enforcement_body: Ticaret Bakanligi (Ministry of Trade) + Tuketici koruma
key_requirements: Lif bilesimine gore etiket zorunlu (%); harmonized fiber names (EU-aligned); care label symbols; Turkish language required; penalties for non-compliance; TURKAK accredited lab testing
notes: Turkey aligns textile labelling with EU standards; customs union with EU facilitates alignment; CE equivalence not required for textiles

regulation_name: DSTU EN ISO 3758:2018 - Textile care labelling
key_requirements: Care labelling symbols for textile articles; adoption of international ISO 3758 standard; mandatory care instructions; fibre content disclosure; Ukrainian language for domestic market
notes: Ukraine EU association agreement aligns standards; war context (2022+) disrupts enforcement but legal requirements remain

key_requirements: Mandatory fiber content disclosure on label (% by weight); required label elements: fiber name, % content, manufacturer/importer name (or RN/WPL number), country where processed/manufactured; advertising rules; penalties up to $51,744 per violation
notes: Core US federal textile labelling law; FTC enforcement; country of origin = where fabric was cut and sewn; synthetics (polyester/nylon/spandex) must use prescribed generic names

key_requirements: Mandatory care instructions for wearing apparel and fabrics (washing, bleaching, drying, ironing, dry cleaning); instructions must be based on testing; permanent label required; follow-up testing obligation; penalties for non-compliance
notes: Permanent care label required sewn into garment; sport legging = wearing apparel in scope; FTC Guide to Textile Fiber Names also relevant

key_requirements: Mandatory flammability standard for clothing textiles; Class 1 = acceptable for use; Class 2 (intermediate) = acceptable for plain surfaces only; Class 3 = rapid and intense burning = prohibited; testing required; CPSC import surveillance
notes: Synthetic sport leggings typically meet Class 1 but must be tested; CPSC can detain/destroy non-compliant imports; recall risk if non-compliant; separate standard for children sleepwear not applicable here

key_requirements: Chemical substance regulations; TSCA Section 6 rules can restrict chemicals in articles including textiles; PFAS reporting rule (40 CFR Part 705) requires manufacturers/importers to report PFAS manufactured/imported since 2011; EPA PFAS strategic roadmap 2021-2024 includes textile sector
notes: EPA PFAS reporting rule under TSCA requires one-time reporting by Jan 2025 for PFAS in articles imported since 2011; sport textile with DWR PFAS finishes subject to reporting; ongoing EPA PFAS regulatory actions

regulation_name: UFLPA - Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act
enforcement_body: CBP (Customs and Border Protection) + UFLPA Entity List
key_requirements: Rebuttable presumption that goods mined/produced/manufactured wholly or in part in Xinjiang (or by entities on UFLPA Entity List) are made with forced labor; importation prohibited unless clear and convincing evidence rebuts presumption; annual UFLPA Entity List updates
notes: Directly impacts synthetic textile supply chain: polyester (China)/cotton (Xinjiang)/yarn/fabric manufactured in China; UFLPA Entity List includes Chinese textile manufacturers; CBP detentions of sportswear significant 2022-2025; supply chain traceability documentation essential

enforcement_body: CalEPA + DTSC (Dept of Toxic Substances Control)
key_requirements: Prohibition on sale of new textile articles containing intentionally added PFAS; effective January 1, 2025; applies to all textile articles sold in California; DWR/stain-resistant finishes most impacted; civil penalties up to $10,000/day
notes: Among first US state PFAS textile bans; effective Jan 2025; intentionally added PFAS definition; sport legging with DWR/antimicrobial PFAS finish = non-compliant; lab testing for total fluorine as screening method

regulation_name: Proposition 65 - Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act
regulation_ref: California Health and Safety Code 25249.5 et seq.
enforcement_body: CA OEHHA + CA AG + Private enforcers (60-day notice)
key_requirements: Warning requirement before knowingly exposing any individual to a listed chemical (Prop 65 list >900 chemicals); textile chemicals of concern: formaldehyde (skin contact), antimony (catalyst in PET production), PFAS (some listed), certain dyes; warning label or reformulation required
notes: Prop 65 list updated quarterly; antimony trioxide (PET catalyst) = listed reproductive toxicant; formaldehyde wrinkle-resistant finishes = listed carcinogen; PFAS additions ongoing; private plaintiff enforcement significant in textiles

regulation_name: Fashion Sustainability and Social Accountability Act (S7428/A8352)
key_requirements: Would require large fashion retailers/manufacturers (>$100M global revenue) selling in NY to map supply chain top 50% by spend; disclose social/environmental impact; set and meet reduction targets; due diligence requirements; civil penalties up to 2% annual revenues
notes: Not yet enacted as of May 2026; reintroduced multiple sessions; significant industry opposition; if enacted = most impactful US state fashion due diligence law; monitor legislative progress

regulation_name: NY Hazardous Substances Disclosure - PFAS in Textiles
key_requirements: Disclosure requirements for PFAS-containing products sold in New York; textile-specific PFAS restrictions under development; reporting of intentional PFAS use in textile products; timeline: disclosure 2024, restriction 2025+
notes: NY PFAS in textiles regulation evolving; complements CA AB 1817; NY DEC guidance for textile manufacturers; legging sport with PFAS finishes directly impacted

regulation_name: Textile Labelling Act + Care Labelling Regulations
regulation_ref: R.S.C. 1985, c. T-10 + SOR/71-272 (Care Labelling) + SOR/2014-148
key_requirements: Mandatory disclosure of fibre content (generic name, % by mass); French and English bilingual labelling for consumer textiles; dealer name/postal code; country of origin if stated must be accurate; care instructions using ASTM D5489 symbols; penalties up to $25,000
notes: Bilingual (EN/FR) labelling = key Canada-specific requirement; care labelling revised 2014 to adopt North American standard symbols; Competition Bureau enforces

regulation_ref: R.S.C. 1985, c. H-3 + Prohibition of Certain Toxic Substances Regulations (SOR/2012-285 amended 2022)
key_requirements: Prohibition on manufacture/use/sale of certain PFAS (PFOA, PFOS, LC-PFCA and related substances) in consumer products; PFAS strategy 2023 extends scope; textiles with PFAS finishes subject to prohibition if containing prohibited substances
notes: Canada PFAS management plan 2023; PFOA/PFOS prohibited; ongoing assessment of additional PFAS under CEPA; DWR PFAS finishes in sport textiles under scrutiny

regulation_name: NOM-004-SE-2021 - Informacion comercial de productos textiles
key_requirements: Etiquetado obligatorio productos textiles: denominacion generica fibras textiles; porcentaje en masa; instrucciones conservacion (simbolos ISO 3758 o texto); datos del responsable; pais de origen; idioma espanol; NOM actualiza version 2015; certificacion por OC acreditado para importaciones
notes: NOM-004-SE-2021 publicada 2022 en vigor; importadores deben certificar conformidad via OC acreditado ante EMA; Profeco hace vigilancia mercado

key_requirements: Regulamento de avaliacao da conformidade para artigos texteis; etiqueta de composicao de fibras obrigatoria; instrucoes de conservacao (simbolos ABNT NBR ISO 3758); fabricante/importador identificado; idioma portugues; certificacao por OCP acreditado para determinados produtos
notes: Portaria 332/2024 atualiza normativa; OCP acreditado INMETRO para certificacao; legging sintetico = artigo de vestuario em escopo

key_requirements: Voluntary standard for sizing of apparel in Brazil; reference for consumer information; recommended size labelling conventions; sport legging sizing information best practice
notes: Voluntary standard; market practice expects compliance; INMETRO may reference in mandatory regulation

regulation_ref: Resolucion 850/2019 de la Secretaria de Comercio Interior
key_requirements: Etiquetado de indumentaria: composicion de fibras (denominacion + porcentaje); fabricante/importador con datos completos; instrucciones de cuidado (texto o simbolos IRAM); pais de fabricacion; idioma castellano; talla obligatorio en punto de venta
notes: Resolucion 850 es la norma vigente; DNCI fiscaliza; importadores deben certificar conformidad

regulation_name: SERNAC - Ley 19.496 de Proteccion al Consumidor + Decreto 97 textiles
key_requirements: Informacion obligatoria en etiqueta: composicion textil; instrucciones de cuidado; fabricante o importador; pais de origen si se menciona; idioma espanol; SERNAC puede sancionar publicidad enganosa o claims medioambientales no verificados
notes: DS 97/2001 especifica requisitos etiquetado ropa; SERNAC activo en greenwashing claims moda sostenible; Chile no tiene REP textile aun

key_requirements: Informacion obligatoria: composicion textil (fibras + porcentajes); instrucciones conservacion; talla; fabricante/importador con domicilio; idioma espanol; SIC puede imponer multas por incumplimiento; inspecciones mercado
notes: SIC es la autoridad de control; importadores inscritos ante DIAN; legging = articulo textil/indumentaria en alcance

regulation_name: MERCOSUR GMC/RES - Rotulagem de artigos texteis
key_requirements: Harmonizacao regional rotulagem textil para Brasil/Argentina/Paraguai/Uruguai; composicao fibras; instrucoes conservacao; dados fabricante/importador; pais de origem; idiomas nacionais (portugues/espanhol); base para normas nacionais RES 850 (AR) e INMETRO (BR)
notes: Resolucao GMC harmoniza base minima; cada pais mantem especificidades nacionais; INMETRO/IRAM podem ter requisitos adicionais

regulation_ref: GB 18401-2010 (Safety Requirements for National Textile Products)
enforcement_body: SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation) + Customs
key_requirements: Mandatory national standard for textile safety; parameters: formaldehyde content (<=75mg/kg for direct skin contact), pH value (4.0-8.5), color fastness (>=3 for direct skin contact), forbidden azo dyes (24 carcinogenic aromatic amines, <20mg/kg), odor assessment; Class B = directly adjacent to skin
notes: Legging sport = Class B (directly adjacent to skin); formaldehyde limit 75mg/kg; 24 banned azo dyes per Annex C; SAMR active enforcement; export-oriented manufacturers increasingly target EU REACH alignment simultaneously

key_requirements: Fibre content disclosure requirements for Chinese domestic market; % composition; Chinese standard fibre names; tolerances; basis for domestic product labelling; aligns with ISO/IEC international standards
notes: GB/T = recommended standard (not mandatory like GB) but widely adopted; domestic Chinese market labelling; export products may use international standards

regulation_ref: Trade Descriptions Ordinance Cap. 362 + Textile Articles Schedule
key_requirements: Prohibited trade descriptions on textile goods; fibre content must be accurately described; false/misleading descriptions = offence; textile articles: wool content declarations; care instruction accuracy; country of origin if stated must be accurate; penalties up to HK$500,000 + imprisonment
notes: HK is a key re-export hub; TDO enforcement by C&ED; legging sold to HK consumers must have accurate labels; no mandatory care label requirement per se but false claim = offence

regulation_name: CNS Textile Labelling - Commodity Labelling Act
regulation_ref: Commodity Labelling Act (commercial label law) + CNS L2039
enforcement_body: BSMI (Bureau of Standards Metrology and Inspection)
key_requirements: Mandatory labelling: product name; fibre content (% by weight); size; care instructions (symbols per CNS); manufacturer/importer with contact; country of origin; Traditional Chinese required; BSMI product inspection for certain categories
notes: BSMI administers commodity labelling; Traditional Chinese mandatory; legging = garment category; some textile categories require BSMI surveillance inspection

regulation_name: Household Goods Quality Labelling Act - Textiles
regulation_ref: Kaji Yohin no Hinshitsu Hyoji Nikannsuru Horitsu (Act No. 104/1962, amended 2014)
key_requirements: Mandatory quality labelling for household goods including apparel; fibre content by % (Japanese JIS names); care instructions (JIS L 0001 symbols); dry cleaning symbols; country of origin; all in Japanese; JIS L 0001:2014 updated symbols aligned with ISO 3758:2012
notes: CAA-mandated quality labelling; JIS L 0001 symbols mandatory since 2016; legging = apparel in scope; Japanese fibre generic names required; Country of origin must be accurate

regulation_name: Act on Control of Household Products Containing Harmful Substances
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW)
key_requirements: Restrictions on harmful substances in household products; listed substances for textiles: DMDM hydantoin, BHT, formaldehyde (<75mg/kg for apparel), organic mercury compounds, tributyltin compounds, triphenyltin compounds; testing requirements; manufacturing/import restrictions
notes: MHLW Designated Chemical Substances; formaldehyde limit for adult apparel = 75ppm; infant clothing limit 16ppm; synthetic sportswear may use finishes (antimicrobial, water repellency) - check against restricted list

regulation_name: Quality Management and Safety Control - KC Common Safety Standard (Apparel)
regulation_ref: Act on Quality Management and Safety Control of Industrial Products + KC Common Safety Standard for Apparel
enforcement_body: KATS (Korean Agency for Technology and Standards) + MOEL
key_requirements: KC Common Safety Standard for apparel: restricted substances (formaldehyde, pH, colour fastness, banned azo dyes per KS K 0650, chromium VI, cadmium, nickel, lead, organotin); mandatory KC mark not required for general adult apparel but must meet standard; supplier confirmation required
notes: KC Common Safety Standard aligns with EU approach; legging = adult apparel; annual KATS market surveillance; formaldehyde <75mg/kg for next-to-skin

regulation_name: K-REACH - Act on Registration and Evaluation of Chemicals
regulation_ref: Act No. 11789 - Chemicals Registration and Evaluation Act (K-REACH)
key_requirements: Registration requirements for substances manufactured/imported >1 ton/year; SVHC-equivalent list (허가대상물질); restrictions list mirrors EU approach; information in supply chain; articles with intentional release of restricted substances covered; PFAS under review
notes: K-REACH requires substance registration; for textile articles intentional release of restricted chemicals covered; K-PFAS restrictions in development; supply chain chemical communication required

enforcement_body: CCCS (Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore)
key_requirements: Prohibition on unfair trade practices including false/misleading representations; fabric composition claims must be accurate; care labelling accuracy; greenwashing claims (eco-friendly, sustainable) subject to fair trading rules; no mandatory textile labelling regulation in Singapore
notes: No mandatory fibre content/care label regulation in Singapore; CPTFA covers misleading claims; retailers self-regulate; SPRING Singapore guidelines are voluntary

key_requirements: Prohibition on false product descriptions; trade descriptions accuracy for textiles; no mandatory textile-specific labelling regulation; Ministry can issue product safety orders; misleading claims on fibre content or sustainability = offence
notes: No specific textile labelling regulation; Consumer Protection Act general prohibition on misdescription; SIRIM voluntary standards exist; export market requirements (EU/US) drive compliance

regulation_name: Consumer Protection Act + Hazardous Substance Act
regulation_ref: Consumer Protection Act B.E.2522 (1979) + Hazardous Substance Act B.E.2535 (1992) amended
enforcement_body: OCPB (Office of the Consumer Protection Board) + DOI
key_requirements: Consumer Protection: accurate labelling requirements; Hazardous Substance Act: restrictions on hazardous chemicals in products; PFAS classified as hazardous substance type; textile chemical restrictions enforced via import controls; no mandatory textile-specific labelling standard yet
notes: Thailand developing textile regulations; hazardous substance controls impact DWR-treated sport textiles; PFAS control measures under review by DOI; export compliance drives manufacturer practice

regulation_ref: Decree No. 43/2017/ND-CP dated April 14, 2017 (amended by Decree 111/2021)
enforcement_body: Ministry of Industry and Trade (MOIT) + STAMEQ
key_requirements: Mandatory labelling requirements for goods including textiles: product name; origin; technical specifications (fibre composition for textiles); Vietnamese language required; manufacturer/importer details; quantity; warning labels if applicable
notes: Vietnam is major textile manufacturer and domestic market growing; Decree 43 general goods labelling applies to textiles; QCVN (national technical regulations) for specific textile safety under development

key_requirements: Mandatory labelling for textile products sold in Indonesia: fibre composition (Bahasa Indonesia + Indonesian standard fibre names); care instructions (SNI symbols); size; manufacturer/importer name and address in Indonesia; SNI alignment; halal certification if marketing claim made
notes: Indonesia is world major textile producer; Permendag 25/2022 current regulation; SNI for textile chemical safety (SNI 7617 for restriction of azo dyes); import supervision by Kemendag; Bahasa Indonesia required on labels

regulation_name: DTI-BPS PNS Care Labelling - Textile Products
key_requirements: Philippine National Standard for care labelling of textile articles; adoption of AS/NZS 1957 standard; voluntary but widely referenced; consumer act prohibits misleading labelling; DTI surveillance of textile imports
notes: PNS voluntary standard; Consumer Act R.A. 7394 prohibits false labelling; DTI-BPS can make PNS mandatory via DAO; import clearance requirements for textile products

regulation_name: BIS IS 17266 - Textile Labelling + Legal Metrology Rules
regulation_ref: IS 17266:2019 (Labelling of Textile Products) + Legal Metrology (Packaged Commodities) Rules 2011
enforcement_body: BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards) + DPIIT Legal Metrology
key_requirements: Textile labelling: fibre composition; country of origin; manufacturer details; size; care instructions (BIS symbols); Legal Metrology PC Rules: MRP, net content, manufacturer name/address, month/year of manufacture on packaged goods; BIS certification mandatory for certain technical textile categories
notes: Legal Metrology rules = strict compliance for packaged textiles sold in India; BIS IS 17266 voluntary but widely adopted; legging in retail packaging = packaged commodity; import clearance requires label compliance

regulation_ref: Trade Practices (Consumer Product Safety Standard) (Care Labelling of Clothing and Household Textiles) Regulations 1991 (updated)
key_requirements: Mandatory care labelling standard for clothing and household textiles; symbols per AS/NZS 1957; all clothing sold in Australia must have permanent care label; label must be legible and durable; ACCC can issue recalls for non-compliant products; penalties up to AUD 50,000 per contravention
notes: AS/NZS 1957:2010 is the reference standard; permanently attached label required; care instructions must be testable and accurate; ACCC publishes compliance guidance; import inspections by Australian Border Force

enforcement_body: Australian Border Force + Dept of Home Affairs
key_requirements: Mandatory annual modern slavery statements for entities with consolidated revenue >=AUD 100M operating in Australia; statements must describe: supply chain structure; modern slavery risks; due diligence actions; effectiveness assessment; garment sector = high risk
notes: Annual reporting to online register; free-text format; DJSSI reviews statements; no enforcement penalty for statement non-compliance currently (review 2023 recommended civil penalties); supply chain: cotton/polyester/nylon from high-risk countries

regulation_ref: Fair Trading Act 1986 + Product Safety Regulations (Care Labelling) (referencing AS/NZS 1957)
enforcement_body: MBIE (Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment) + Commerce Commission
key_requirements: Care labelling for textile articles sold in NZ; mandatory care symbols per AS/NZS 1957:2010; accurate instructions; Fair Trading Act prohibits misleading representations; care label accuracy required; permanent attachment required
notes: NZ aligns with Australia on AS/NZS 1957; jointly maintained standard; Commerce Commission enforces Fair Trading Act; no separate NZ textile fibre content regulation but misleading labels = FTA offence

regulation_name: ASEAN - No harmonised textile regulation (national rules apply)
regulation_ref: ASEAN Trade in Goods Agreement (ATIGA) + national regulations
key_requirements: No harmonised ASEAN textile labelling regulation; ASEAN Cosmetic Directive does NOT apply to textiles; ATIGA reduces tariffs but does not harmonise product standards; each member state applies national textile regulations; ASEAN Working Group on Standards and Conformance addresses alignment
notes: No bloc-level mandatory textile standard; each ASEAN country (SG/MY/TH/VN/ID/PH etc.) has national rules; reference individual country entries for applicable regulations

key_requirements: Mandatory labelling for textile products in GCC countries: fibre content (Arabic language mandatory + English optional); care instructions (symbols per GSO/ISO); country of origin; manufacturer/importer details; size; GSO certification/registration required for many categories; SASO type approval for Saudi market
notes: GSO standards apply across Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman; SASO most stringent (type approval system); Arabic labels mandatory; care symbols per GSO aligned with ISO 3758; legging = apparel in scope

key_requirements: GSO standards on restricted chemical substances in textiles: formaldehyde limits, banned azo dyes, pH requirements; aligned with international approach (OEKO-TEX, REACH Annex XVII); testing required for certification; heavy metals restrictions
notes: Chemical restrictions aligned with global approach; lab testing required for conformity; growing enforcement in UAE/Saudi markets

regulation_name: Standards Institution of Israel SI 5466 - Textiles
regulation_ref: SI 5466 (Textile products - general safety and labelling)
enforcement_body: ILAC + SII (Standards Institution of Israel) + Ministry of Economy
key_requirements: Mandatory standard for textile products safety and labelling in Israel; fibre content; care instructions; restricted substances (formaldehyde, azo dyes, pH); Hebrew mandatory; Standard Mark for applicable categories; legging = apparel category
notes: Israel has mandatory standards for textiles requiring SII certification for some categories; Hebrew labelling required; importers must obtain import license/permit; PFAS provisions being assessed

regulation_name: Egyptian Organization for Standardization ES 5366 - Textiles
enforcement_body: GOEIC (General Organization for Export and Import Control)
key_requirements: Pre-shipment inspection required for textile imports; GOEIC conformity certificate; Egyptian Standard ES for textile labelling: fibre content, care instructions, Arabic language; restricted substances aligned with international standards; SONCAP-equivalent conformity assessment
notes: GOEIC mandatory pre-shipment inspection for textile imports; Egyptian Standard adoption of ISO/EN standards; Arabic labelling required; delays at customs common; legging = garment category

regulation_name: Loi 24-09 - Securite des produits et services
regulation_ref: Loi n. 24-09 relative a la securite des produits et des services (Maroc)
key_requirements: Securite generale des produits places sur le marche marocain; conformite aux normes marocaines (NM); etiquetage en arabe et francais; importateurs responsables de la conformite; IMANOR publie NM pour textiles; reference aux standards internationaux ISO/EN
notes: Maroc EU Association Agreement facilitates alignment; IMANOR NM aligns with ISO/EN textile standards; Arabic+French labelling; growing enforcement

regulation_ref: SANS 10146:2019 + Compulsory Specification VC 8014 (Care Labelling of Textile Articles)
enforcement_body: NRCS (National Regulator for Compulsory Specifications) + ITAC
key_requirements: Mandatory care labelling for textile articles sold in South Africa; care symbols per SANS 10146 (aligned with ISO 3758); permanent label required; NRCS letter of authority (LoA) required for importers; testing by SABS accredited lab; penalties for non-compliant imports
notes: VC 8014 is compulsory specification = mandatory; NRCS LoA required before import; ITAC import permit system; SABS SANS 10146 aligned with ISO 3758; legging = apparel in scope

regulation_ref: SON (Standards Organisation of Nigeria) + NAFDAC for chemical content
key_requirements: SON mandatory standards for textile products; NIS (Nigerian Industrial Standards) for textile labelling; NAFDAC oversight for chemical content in consumer goods; SONCAP (Standards Organisation of Nigeria Conformity Assessment Programme) mandatory for imports; SON Mark for domestic products
notes: SONCAP mandatory for textile imports from non-ECOWAS countries; NIS standards cover fibre content/care labelling; NAFDAC may have jurisdiction if textile product makes cosmetic/health claims; legging as sport apparel = standard textile

regulation_name: KEBS PVoC - Pre-Export Verification of Conformity
regulation_ref: Kenya Bureau of Standards (KEBS) + PVoC programme
key_requirements: Pre-export verification of conformity (PVoC) mandatory for regulated products exported to Kenya; textile products on PVoC list require KEBS inspection certificate before shipment; compliance with KS standards for textile labelling; fibre content; care labelling; chemical restrictions
notes: KEBS PVoC issued at origin country; textile importers must work with KEBS-appointed inspection agents (SGS, Bureau Veritas, Intertek); KS standards align with ISO; legging = consumer textile in PVoC scope

regulation_ref: N/A - Codex scope limited to food and feed products
key_requirements: Codex standards apply exclusively to food and feed products; sport textile/legging = not in scope of Codex
notes: Codex does not regulate textile products; no application to legging sport synthetique

regulation_ref: ISO 3758:2012 (Textiles - Care labelling code using symbols)
key_requirements: International standard for care labelling symbols; 5 symbol groups: washing, bleaching, tumble drying, ironing, professional textile care; adopted by EU (EN ISO 3758), Japan (JIS L 0001), Australia (AS/NZS 1957), and 50+ countries; voluntary international standard but basis for mandatory national regulations
notes: ISO 3758 = foundational global care labelling standard; virtually universal adoption via national mandatories; 2012 version current; compliance achieves simultaneous multi-market compliance

regulation_name: UNECE WP.29 - Not Applicable (automotive regulations only)
regulation_ref: N/A - UNECE WP.29 scope: vehicle regulations
enforcement_body: UNECE WP.29 (World Forum for Harmonization of Vehicle Regulations)
key_requirements: UNECE WP.29 develops vehicle technical regulations; not applicable to textile consumer products
notes: UNECE WP.29 = vehicle safety/performance regulations; no application to sport textile legging

regulation_name: WADA - Not Applicable (sport apparel does not contain prohibited substances)
key_requirements: WADA Prohibited List regulates substances taken by athletes; sport clothing/textile as external apparel is not regulated by WADA unless containing transdermal drug delivery system
notes: WADA not applicable to sport legging as passive apparel; unless textile incorporated drug delivery patches - not the case for standard synthetic legging

regulation_name: OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains in Garment & Footwear Sector
regulation_ref: OECD (2017) Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains in the Garment and Footwear Sector
key_requirements: Voluntary guidance for companies sourcing garments/footwear; 5-step due diligence framework: embed RBC; identify and assess supply chain risks; cease/prevent/mitigate risks; track; communicate; applies to all tiers of supply chain; aligned with UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights
notes: OECD Guidance used as reference by CSDDD/LkSG/CSRD for expected due diligence practices; garment sector = one of first sectors with specific OECD sector guidance; widely referenced by French Loi Vigilance and German LkSG implementation guidance

regulation_ref: Regulation (EU) 2016/425 of the European Parliament and of the Council
enforcement_body: European Commission DG GROW + National Market Surveillance Authorities
key_requirements: EPI catégorie II: conformity assessment Module B (EU-type examination by Notified Body) + Module C, D or E for production; CE marking mandatory; Declaration of Performance; harmonised standard EN 1078 applies; importer/distributor obligations; market surveillance
notes: PPE Regulation repealed Directive 89/686/EEC since April 2018; EPI catégorie II = risques intermédiaires; casque vélo adulte = catégorie II; Module B + C/D/E mandatory; Notified Body required

regulation_name: EN 1078:2012+A1:2012 — Helmets for pedal cyclists and users of skateboards and roller skates
key_requirements: Harmonised standard specifying performance requirements: field of vision; retention system effectiveness (dynamic strength test); shock absorption (drop test); roll-off test; area of protection; labelling requirements (CE mark, EN 1078 reference, size, manufacturer, year of manufacture); test methods; presumption of conformity with PPE Regulation
notes: EN 1078 = mandatory harmonised standard for bicycle helmets in EU; compliance = presumption of conformity with EU 2016/425; certification by Notified Body required for Module B; standard covers pedal cyclists and roller skate users (adults); children sub-spec same standard

regulation_name: Module B+C/D/E — Notified Body conformity assessment
key_requirements: Module B: EU-type examination by Notified Body; technical documentation; sample testing to EN 1078; NB issues EU-type examination certificate; Module C/D/E: production conformity; quality assurance or product verification; NB surveillance; Declaration of Conformity; CE marking; RAPEX notification if non-compliant
notes: Module B mandatory for EPI cat. II; choice of Module C, D or E for production phase; NB must be designated for PPE; certificates valid 5 years; NB list on NANDO database

enforcement_body: European Commission + National Market Surveillance Authorities
key_requirements: General product safety requirements complementing sector legislation; economic operator obligations; online marketplace requirements; product traceability; serious risk reporting; RAPEX/Safety Gate notifications; responsible person in EU required
notes: GPSR complements PPE Regulation for aspects not covered; traceability and market surveillance strengthened; online sales explicitly covered; applicable from 13 December 2024

regulation_name: Décret n°2016-364 — Port du casque obligatoire enfants < 12 ans
regulation_ref: Décret n°2016-364 du 28 mars 2016 (Code de la route, art. R431-1)
enforcement_body: Ministère de l'Intérieur + DSR (Délégation à la Sécurité Routière)
key_requirements: Port du casque à vélo obligatoire pour les enfants de moins de 12 ans en tant que passager ou conducteur; casque doit être homologué (conforme EN 1078/CE); sanctions: amende contravention 2ème classe; vendeurs doivent informer l'acheteur; recommandation pour adultes mais non obligatoire
notes: Obligation pour -12 ans seulement; adultes: port recommandé non obligatoire; casque doit être CE/EN 1078; décret modificatif Code de la route; DSR campagnes de sensibilisation annuelles

regulation_name: DGCCRF — Contrôle du marché EPI catégorie II
regulation_ref: Règlement (UE) 2016/425 + Code de la consommation L221-1 et s.
enforcement_body: DGCCRF (Direction Générale de la Concurrence, de la Consommation et de la Répression des Fraudes)
key_requirements: Surveillance du marché: contrôles des casques vélo CE; vérification de la conformité NB; étiquetage; déclaration de conformité; retrait/rappel si non-conforme; sanctions administratives et pénales; RAPEX notifications
notes: DGCCRF effectue campagnes de contrôle cyclistes; fréquentes non-conformités détectées (étiquetage, absence DdC, faux CE); opération de surveillance marché annuelle

enforcement_body: BAuA (Bundesanstalt für Arbeitsschutz und Arbeitsmedizin) + Länder market surveillance
key_requirements: ProdSG transposes GPSD/GPSR; EU 2016/425 directly applicable; market surveillance by Länder authorities; ECHA/RAPEX reporting; GS-Zeichen (voluntary safety mark) prevalent in DE market; EN 1078 mandatory via EU 2016/425; retailer obligations under ProdSG
notes: Germany: EU 2016/425 directly applicable; ProdSG = framework national law; Länder (states) conduct market surveillance; GS-Zeichen (Geprüfte Sicherheit) highly valued by German consumers even if voluntary; DE market: adult helmet use recommended, not mandated (except some Bundesland cycling routes)

regulation_name: D.Lgs 475/1992 + Reg. UE 2016/425 — DPI Categoria II
enforcement_body: MISE (Ministero delle Imprese e del Made in Italy) + Guardia di Finanza
key_requirements: EU 2016/425 directly applicable in Italy; D.Lgs 475/1992 older DPI framework largely superseded; market surveillance by MISE; EN 1078 required; IT: Codice della Strada — casque obligatoire < 14 ans à vélo (Art. 68 CdS révisé 2023); adults encouraged not mandated
notes: Italy 2023 revision Code de la route: casque obligatoire enfants <14 ans à vélo; adultes non obligatoire; CE/EN 1078 conformity required for sale; MISE market surveillance active post-2023 reform

regulation_name: Real Decreto 1428/2003 — Casque obligatoire circulation interurbaine
regulation_ref: Real Decreto 1428/2003 por el que se aprueba el Reglamento General de Circulación
key_requirements: Port du casque à vélo obligatoire hors agglomération (interurbain) pour tous les cyclistes; en agglomération: recommandé; <16 ans: obligatoire même en agglomération; casque doit être homologué CE/EN 1078; DGT campagnes; amendes infraction Code de la route
notes: ES: casque obligatoire hors agglomération tous âges; <16 ans: obligatoire partout; proposition d'étendre l'obligation en ville en discussion; vente: conformité EU 2016/425 + EN 1078 mandatory

key_requirements: EU 2016/425 directly applicable; NVWA market surveillance; EN 1078 required; NL: pas d'obligation légale port du casque cycliste (adultes ni enfants) sauf règles de sécurité professionnelles; casque PPE cat. II requis si utilisation EPI au travail; vente: conformité CE mandatory
notes: Netherlands: aucune obligation légale port casque cycliste (culturellement: vélo sans casque majoritaire); vente = EU 2016/425 applicable; NVWA contrôle conformité CE; occupation PPE use: additional BetrSichV-equivalent rules

regulation_ref: Personal Protective Equipment (Enforcement) Regulations 2018 (SI 2018/390) + UK PPE Regulation (retained EU law amended)
enforcement_body: OPSS (Office for Product Safety and Standards) + HSE (Health and Safety Executive)
key_requirements: Post-Brexit UK legislation: retained and amended EU 2016/425 into UK law; UKCA marking mandatory (replacing CE for GB market from Jan 2025 for new products); UK Approved Body assessment; BS EN 1078 required; Declaration of Conformity; economic operator obligations; NI: CE still accepted (Windsor Framework)
notes: UKCA mandatory for GB (England, Scotland, Wales) from 1 Jan 2025 for new PPE; CE accepted for NI market; UK Approved Body (formerly NB) required; BS EN 1078 identical to EN 1078; important: dual marking CE+UKCA possible for dual market; OPSS enforcement

regulation_ref: BS EN 1078:2012+A1:2012 (identical to EN 1078, adopted by BSI)
key_requirements: UK adopted standard for bicycle helmets; identical technical content to EN 1078:2012+A1:2012; referenced under UK PPE Regulations 2018; compliance gives presumption of conformity with UK PPE requirements; UK Approved Body testing required for Module B equivalent
notes: BSI BS EN 1078 = technically identical to CEN EN 1078; harmonised standard under UK PPE Regulations; UKCA marking requires UK Approved Body certificate

regulation_name: SR 930.115 — Ordonnance sur les EPI (Swiss PPE Ordinance)
regulation_ref: Ordonnance du DEFR sur les équipements de protection individuelle (OEPInd, SR 930.115)
key_requirements: Switzerland has bilaterally aligned with EU 2016/425 via MRA (Mutual Recognition Agreement); CE marking accepted; EN 1078 required; CH-specific: Mutual Recognition Agreement CH-EU on conformity assessment; Notified Body certificate from EU NB accepted; SECO market surveillance; Loi fédérale sur la sécurité des produits (LSIP)
notes: CH-EU MRA means CE mark + EU NB certificate accepted in CH; no separate CH approval needed; SECO market surveillance; LSIP = loi-cadre nationale; casque vélo adulte: non obligatoire par loi (recommandé); enfants: obligation cantonale dans certains cantons

regulation_ref: Regulation (EU) 2016/425 as incorporated into Norwegian law via EEA Agreement
key_requirements: Norway = EEA member; EU 2016/425 fully applicable via EEA; EN 1078 required; CE marking; Notified Body; DSB market surveillance; Norwegian road traffic law: hjelm anbefalt men ikke påbudt for voksne (recommended not mandatory); Vegtrafikkloven § and regulations
notes: Norway EEA: EU 2016/425 fully applicable; no separate Norwegian PPE regulation needed; DSB = competent authority; helmet mandatory for motorcycles (mopeds) but cycling: recommended only for adults; CE/EN 1078 mandatory for sale

regulation_ref: TR TS 019/2011 «О безопасности средств индивидуальной защиты» (EAEU Technical Regulation)
enforcement_body: Rospotrebnadzor + Federal Agency on Technical Regulating and Metrology (Rosstandart)
key_requirements: EAEU Technical Regulation TR TS 019/2011 mandatory for PPE including protective helmets; EAC mark (Евразийское соответствие) required; certification by accredited EAEU certification body; conformity assessment: type test + quality system surveillance; Russian GOST standards referenced; GOST 28889 (protective helmets); import permit
notes: TR TS 019/2011 = mandatory EAEU regulation covering all PPE including sports helmets; EAC mark required; certification in Russia or EAEU country; validity: 1-5 years; importers/manufacturers must register; separate from EU system; GOST R harmonised standards

regulation_ref: KKD Yönetmeliği (7518 sayılı Kişisel Koruyucu Donanım Yönetmeliği, 2016/425/EU transpoze)
enforcement_body: Çalışma ve Sosyal Güvenlik Bakanlığı (Ministry of Labour and Social Security)
key_requirements: Turkey transposed EU 2016/425 via KKD Yönetmeliği; CE marking accepted; EN 1078 referenced; Turkish Standards (TS EN 1078) adopted; conformity assessment: Notified Body equivalent or CE from EU NB accepted via Customs Union; market surveillance by Ministry of Labour; TÜRKAK accreditation body; import controls
notes: Turkey-EU Customs Union: CE mark accepted for many products; KKD Yönetmeliği transposed EU 2016/425; TS EN 1078 identical to EN 1078; EU NB certificate may be accepted; additional ÜRÜGE system for market surveillance; helmet: no mandatory use law for adult cyclists currently

regulation_ref: Постанова КМУ №761 «Про затвердження Технічного регламенту засобів індивідуального захисту» (2017)
enforcement_body: State Service of Ukraine for Food Safety and Consumer Protection + Derzhstandart
key_requirements: Ukraine Association Agreement with EU: technical regulation aligned with EU Directive 89/686/EEC (pre-2016/425 framework); TR ЗІЗ (PPE Technical Regulation) requires conformity marking UA; Ukrainian certification body assessment; EN 1078 referenced; war-context: enforcement reduced but regulations formally in force
notes: UA aligned with EU PPE framework via Association Agreement; pre-2016/425 transposition based on Directive 89/686; UA mark required; war-context 2022-ongoing affects normal market operation; formal regulations in place; Derzhstandart manages standards

regulation_name: TR TS 019/2011 — On the safety of personal protective equipment (EAEU bloc)
regulation_ref: TR TS 019/2011 (Таможенный союз — Технический регламент о безопасности СИЗ)
key_requirements: EAEU bloc-level mandatory technical regulation covering all PPE including sports/cycling helmets; applies to RU, BY, KZ, AM, KG; EAC marking mandatory; type test by accredited body; quality system surveillance; GOST-referenced standards; unified certification valid across all EAEU members; mutual recognition within bloc
notes: TR TS 019/2011 covers full EAEU bloc (Russia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Armenia, Kyrgyzstan); EAC certification obtained in one member state valid for all; test labs must be accredited per EAEU rules; cycling helmet = personal protective equipment in scope

regulation_name: 16 CFR Part 1203 — CPSC Safety Standard for Bicycle Helmets
regulation_ref: 16 CFR Part 1203 (mandatory safety standard under Consumer Product Safety Act)
key_requirements: MANDATORY for all bicycle helmets sold in the US regardless of user age; shock attenuation requirements (flat anvil and hemispherical anvil drop tests, peak g < 300g); retention system tests; coverage requirements; peripheral vision tests; labelling: CPSC certification label required on each helmet; third-party accredited lab testing; General Certificate of Conformity (GCC); civil/criminal penalties for non-compliance
notes: 16 CFR Part 1203 = MANDATORY US federal law; no voluntary option; applies to ALL bicycle helmets sold in US; third-party accredited lab testing required; GCC required; CPSC enforcement; importers and manufacturers equally responsible; non-compliant helmets subject to recall

regulation_name: CPSC — General Certificate of Conformity (GCC) + Third Party Testing
key_requirements: Mandatory third-party testing by CPSC-accredited laboratory before sale; General Certificate of Conformity (GCC) required and must accompany shipment; electronic submission via CPSC system; importer/retailer obligations; recall obligations; undue risk reporting
notes: GCC = mandatory document; must be based on third-party accredited lab test to 16 CFR Part 1203; GCC must be on file and available to CPSC; failure = prohibited hazardous substance; CPSC maintains list of accredited labs

key_requirements: Mandatory helmet law: persons under 18 operating or riding as passenger on bicycle must wear approved helmet; helmet must meet CPSC, ASTM, or Snell standard; parent liability; fine up to $25 + county/city fees; helmet sale must meet 16 CFR 1203 (federal)
notes: CA §21212: mandatory <18 years; adults: no state mandate; some cities/localities may have additional rules; helmet must be approved standard (CPSC preferred); parent/guardian liability for minor violation

regulation_name: NY Vehicle and Traffic Law §1238 — Mandatory helmet under 14
regulation_ref: New York Vehicle and Traffic Law §1238
enforcement_body: New York State DMV + local police
key_requirements: Mandatory helmet for cyclists under 14 years; helmet must meet CPSC or equivalent standard; parent/guardian liable; fine $50; helmet loaner program in some municipalities; NYC additional rules under local law; adult cycling helmet: voluntary but strongly encouraged
notes: NY §1238: mandatory <14 years; NYC Dept of Transportation encourages adult helmet use; no state adult mandate; federal CPSC standard applies to all helmets sold regardless; some NYC cycling lanes have city campaigns

regulation_ref: CSA D113.2-M89 (Canadian Standards Association — Cycling Helmets)
enforcement_body: Health Canada + Transport Canada + Provincial Ministries
key_requirements: CSA D113.2-M89 is reference standard for bicycle helmets in Canada; helmet laws vary by province; CPSC-certified helmets generally accepted in Canada (concurrent compliance); Health Canada product safety oversight; Consumer Product Safety Act (CCPSA) framework; BC, NS, ON, NB, PEI, AB mandatory helmet laws (various age requirements)
notes: Canada: federal framework (CCPSA) + provincial mandatory use laws; BC mandatory all ages; NS mandatory all ages; ON mandatory under 18; NB, PEI mandatory all ages; AB mandatory under 18; QC: no mandatory law; CPSC-certified helmets accepted; importers must comply with CCPSA

regulation_name: Provincial helmet laws — BC, NS, ON, NB, PEI mandatory
regulation_ref: Motor Vehicle Act (BC) s.183 + NS Motor Vehicle Act + ON Highway Traffic Act s.104 + similar provincial laws
key_requirements: BC: mandatory all cyclists all ages; NS: mandatory all cyclists all ages; ON: mandatory under 18; NB: mandatory all cyclists all ages; PEI: mandatory all cyclists all ages; AB: mandatory under 18; SK, MB, QC: no provincial mandate; helmet must meet CSA D113.2 or CPSC 16 CFR 1203; fines vary by province
notes: Provincial patchwork: BC/NS/NB/PEI most stringent (all ages mandatory); retailers must ensure helmets meet CSA or CPSC standard for Canadian market; dual Canada/US products common (both CSA and CPSC compliance)

regulation_ref: NOM-206-SCFI-2002 (Norma Oficial Mexicana — Cascos protectores para uso ciclista)
key_requirements: NOM-206-SCFI-2002 is mandatory standard for cycling helmets sold in Mexico; shock absorption tests; retention system; ventilation; labelling requirements (NOM compliance); NOM-conformity mark; testing by accredited Mexican lab; imported helmets must demonstrate NOM compliance; SE enforcement; PROFECO consumer protection
notes: NOM-206-SCFI-2002 = mandatory for all cycling helmets sold in MX; CPSC or CE-certified helmets do not automatically comply (separate NOM test required in principle); NOM mark required; testing in SINALP-accredited lab; import documentation required; mandatory use: varies by state/municipality

regulation_ref: ABNT NBR 16175:2013 (Capacetes para ciclistas — Requisitos e métodos de ensaio)
key_requirements: INMETRO Portaria 41/2009 establishes compulsory certification for bicycle helmets in Brazil; ABNT NBR 16175 is the referenced technical standard; INMETRO certification mark mandatory; third-party lab testing by INMETRO-accredited body; Declaration of Conformity; importers must register product with INMETRO; heavy penalties for non-compliant products
notes: INMETRO certification = mandatory for all bike helmets sold in BR; ABNT NBR 16175 referenced standard; INMETRO OCP (Organismos de Certificação de Produtos) performs certification; SELO INMETRO required on each helmet; customs clearance requires INMETRO certificate; no automatic recognition of CE/CPSC

regulation_ref: Ley 24.449 (Ley Nacional de Tránsito) + IRAM 3621 (Cascos protectores para ciclistas)
enforcement_body: Ministerio de Transporte + VIALIDAD NACIONAL + IRAM
key_requirements: Ley 24.449 establishes road safety requirements including helmet use; IRAM 3621 is reference standard for cycling helmets; mandatory helmet use laws vary by province (Buenos Aires province mandatory); IRAM certification voluntary but required for public tenders; Argentine standards body IRAM; municipal rules add local obligations
notes: AR: Ley 24.449 framework + provincial implementations; CABA (Buenos Aires city) and Buenos Aires province: mandatory helmet; IRAM 3621 standard for helmets; IRAM mark often required by provincial/municipal procurement; imported helmets: IRAM acceptance or equivalence demonstration

regulation_name: Ley 18.290 de Tránsito — Port du casque obligatoire tous cyclistes
regulation_ref: Ley N°18.290 (Ley de Tránsito, Artículo 196 bis) + Decreto Supremo 207/2000
enforcement_body: CONASET (Comisión Nacional de Seguridad de Tránsito) + Carabineros
key_requirements: Chile: port du casque à vélo OBLIGATOIRE pour tous les cyclistes quel que soit l'âge (loi 18.290 art. 196 bis); casque doit répondre aux normes techniques; Decreto Supremo 207/2000 approuve les conditions techniques des casques; CONASET contrôle; amende pour non-port; normes INN (Instituto Nacional de Normalización)
notes: CL = obligation tous cyclistes adultes et enfants; une des lois les plus strictes Amérique latine; normes INN référencées (NCh équivalences ISO/EN); importateurs doivent démontrer conformité technique; SERNAC contrôle produits

regulation_name: Resolución 1737/2004 — Equipos de protección personal ciclistas
regulation_ref: Resolución 1737 de 2004 (Ministerio de Transporte) + Ley 769/2002 (Código Nacional de Tránsito)
key_requirements: Resolución 1737/2004 establece requisitos técnicos para cascos para ciclistas vendidos en Colombia; Código Nacional de Tránsito Ley 769/2002: uso de casco obligatorio para ciclistas; ICONTEC standards reference (NTC); ONAC accreditation for conformity bodies; Supersalud/Supertransporte oversight
notes: CO: uso de casco OBLIGATORIO para ciclistas bajo el Código de Tránsito; Resolución 1737 técnica referencia; ICONTEC NTC standards; conformidad demostrable mediante certificación por organismo acreditado ONAC; importadores registrar productos

key_requirements: No fully unified MERCOSUR helmet standard; bilateral mutual recognition agreements between member states; INMETRO certification (BR) widely referenced; ABNT NBR 16175 used as de facto reference; IRAM certification (AR) for Argentine market; national certifications prevail; Mercosur working toward harmonisation
notes: MERCOSUR bloc: no single unified cycling helmet standard; each member state maintains national certification system; INMETRO (BR) most rigorous; bilateral MRAs reduce duplication; exporters to MERCOSUR region often obtain INMETRO as primary certification

regulation_name: GB 24429-2009 — Bicycle and electric cycling helmets
regulation_ref: GB 24429-2009 《运动头盔 自行车、滑板、轮滑运动头盔的安全要求和试验方法》
enforcement_body: SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation) + SAC (Standardization Administration of China)
key_requirements: GB 24429-2009 is mandatory safety standard for bicycle/skateboard/roller sports helmets in China; shock absorption performance; retention system strength; ventilation; labelling; Chinese text required; GB national standard = mandatory (强制性标准); testing by CMA-accredited lab required; CCC (China Compulsory Certification) may apply depending on product classification; SAMR market surveillance
notes: GB 24429-2009 = mandatory GB standard for cycling helmets; CCC certification may be required (check product category); CMA-accredited lab test; Chinese labelling mandatory; GB 2626 (respiratory PPE) not applicable; GB 811-2022 = motorcycle helmets (different product); post-2021: SAMR increasing enforcement

regulation_name: CCC — China Compulsory Certification (if applicable to sports helmet category)
regulation_ref: China Compulsory Certification (3C) system under CNCA Decree
enforcement_body: CNCA (Certification and Accreditation Administration of China)
key_requirements: Sports helmets (cycling/skateboard/roller) subject to CCC if classified under mandatory CCC product catalogue; verification required: check current CCC catalogue for bicycle helmet inclusion; if CCC required: CNCA-designated body testing + factory inspection; CCC mark on product; import customs clearance requires CCC certificate
notes: CCC applicability for cycling helmets: check current CNCA catalogue; as of 2024 cycling helmets may be subject to voluntary certification under CVC (China Voluntary Certification) via ChinaGAP/CCGA schemes; SAMR has been updating scope; importers must verify current classification

regulation_name: Consumer Protection — General Safety and Product Liability
regulation_ref: Consumer Goods Safety Ordinance (Cap 456) + Product Liability Ordinance (Cap 187)
enforcement_body: Hong Kong Customs and Excise Department + Consumer Council
key_requirements: No specific mandatory standard for bicycle helmets in HK; Consumer Goods Safety Ordinance applies general safety requirements; CE or CPSC-certified helmets accepted; product liability framework; voluntary EN 1078 or CPSC compliance recommended; no mandatory helmet use law for cyclists in HK
notes: HK: no specific cycling helmet mandatory standard; CGSO general safety applies; CE/CPSC voluntary compliance sufficient; no mandatory helmet law for cyclists; Consumer Council monitors product safety; helmets widely sold with voluntary international certifications

regulation_ref: CNS 13371 (中華民國國家標準 — 自行車安全帽)
enforcement_body: BSMI (Bureau of Standards, Metrology and Inspection)
key_requirements: CNS 13371 is the Taiwan national standard for cycling helmets; BSMI mandatory inspection: type testing; Commercial Standards (商品檢驗) system; BSMI registration required for imported helmets; CNS compliance marking; shock absorption tests; retention system; ventilation requirements; Road Traffic Management and Penalty Act: mandatory helmet use for motorcyclists and cyclists on some roads
notes: TW: BSMI mandatory inspection for cycling helmets under CNS 13371; importers must register and obtain BSMI approval; CNS 13371 test methods aligned with international standards; helmet use: mandatory for certain road categories and ages under traffic law

regulation_name: JIS T 8134 — Protective helmets for cyclists
regulation_ref: JIS T 8134:2018 (自転車用ヘルメット)
enforcement_body: Consumer Affairs Agency (CAA) + METI + NITE (National Institute of Technology and Evaluation)
key_requirements: JIS T 8134 is the primary Japanese standard for cycling helmets; compliance voluntary but industry practice; SG Mark (Safety Goods) certification by PL Insurance Society highly prevalent and de facto required; Road Traffic Law amendment 2023: helmet recommended for ALL cyclists (adults); mandatory for under-13 on some prefectural roads; Consumer Product Safety Act (消費生活用製品安全法) framework
notes: Japan 2023 Road Traffic Law revision: helmet strongly recommended (努力義務) for all cyclists; SG Mark ubiquitous in JP market; JIS T 8134 compliance + SG Mark = minimum market expectation; JIS T 8134 technically aligns with EN 1078; CAA product safety oversight; NITE incident investigation

regulation_name: SG Mark — Safety Goods voluntary certification
regulation_ref: SG Mark system (Product Safety Association + PL Insurance)
enforcement_body: 製品安全協会 (Product Safety Association Japan)
key_requirements: SG Mark = voluntary safety certification covering product liability insurance; de facto industry standard for helmets sold in Japan; SG certification body tests to JIS T 8134; certification valid 3 years; SG mark on product; consumers strongly prefer SG-marked products; retailers often require SG certification from suppliers
notes: SG Mark is effectively required for commercial success in JP market; JIS T 8134 = technical reference; PL insurance bundled with SG certification protects consumer and manufacturer; gap without SG = significant market access barrier

regulation_ref: KS G 6601 (자전거 이용자를 위한 보호 헬멧)
enforcement_body: KATS (Korean Agency for Technology and Standards) + NTSB (National Traffic Safety Board)
key_requirements: KS G 6601 is the Korean industrial standard for cycling helmets; Safety Certification (안전확인) under Consumer Product Safety Act (CPSA) for helmets (youth); Self-Regulatory Safety Confirmation (자율안전확인) for adult helmets; KC mark required for safety-certified products; Road Traffic Act: mandatory helmet use for cyclists
notes: KR: KC mark required for bicycle helmets under CPSA; youth helmets = Safety Certification (stricter); adult helmets = Self-Regulatory Safety Confirmation; KS G 6601 referenced standard; KATS oversight; Road Traffic Act requires helmet use; testing by KTC or other accredited body

regulation_name: IS 4151 — Protective helmets + BIS compulsory certification
regulation_ref: IS 4151:2015 (Protective Helmets) + Bureau of Indian Standards (Compulsory Certification)
enforcement_body: BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards) + Ministry of Consumer Affairs
key_requirements: IS 4151 covers protective helmets in India; BIS compulsory certification (BIS Mark Scheme) applicable to helmets; ISI Mark required on certified helmets; BIS-licensed testing lab; Motor Vehicles Act: two-wheeler helmets mandatory (BIS certification mandatory under CMV Rules for motorcycle helmets); cycling helmets: no explicit mandatory use law for cyclists but IS 4151 reference; BIS CRS (Consumer Safety) scheme
notes: India: two-wheeler motorcycle helmets = mandatory BIS IS 4151 certification; cycling helmets: no explicit mandatory use law nationally (state-level rules vary); BIS compulsory certification for helmets sold as protective equipment; IS 4151 aligned with international standards; BIS license = mandatory for sale

regulation_ref: AS/NZS 2063:2020 (Bicycle helmets — Safety requirements, test methods and labelling)
enforcement_body: Product Safety Australia + State/Territory Road Authorities (Transport for NSW, VicRoads, etc.)
key_requirements: AS/NZS 2063 is MANDATORY for all bicycle helmets sold in Australia; Product Safety Standard under Australian Consumer Law (ACL); shock absorption tests; retention system; area of protection; ventilation; labelling: AS/NZS 2063 compliance required; ACCC enforcement; mandatory helmet use: ALL cyclists in all Australian states/territories by law; non-AS/NZS 2063 helmets cannot be sold
notes: AU: AS/NZS 2063 = mandatory product safety standard (compulsory Product Safety Standard under ACL); ALL cyclists must wear approved helmet in every state; ACCC enforcement; testing by NATA-accredited lab; AS/NZS 2063:2020 is current version (updated from 2008); CE/CPSC helmets do NOT satisfy AU requirement unless also AS/NZS 2063 certified; dual certification common for exporters

enforcement_body: Waka Kotahi NZ Transport Agency + Consumer NZ
key_requirements: NZ Product Safety Standard mandates AS/NZS 2063 for bicycle helmets (jointly maintained with AU); Land Transport (Road User) Rule 2004 requires all cyclists to wear approved helmet; police enforcement; MBIE product safety oversight; fines for non-compliant helmet use or sale; AS/NZS 2063 compliance labelling required
notes: NZ: same mandatory standard as AU (AS/NZS 2063); all cyclists legally required to wear helmet; Product Safety Regulations reference AS/NZS 2063; no CE/CPSC substitution; dual AU/NZ standard maintained by Standards Australia and Standards NZ jointly

regulation_name: ASEAN Mutual Recognition Arrangement for PPE — sector harmonisation
regulation_ref: ASEAN MRA framework + ASEAN Consultative Committee for Standards and Quality (ACCSQ)
