regulation_ref: ISO 28580:2018 (Passenger car truck and bus tyres — Methods of measuring rolling resistance)
key_requirements: International standard for measuring rolling resistance coefficient (RRC); single-point measurement; referenced by EU Reg 2020/740, CN GB 30510, India MoRTH, ECE R117; mandatory for tyre labelling compliance globally
notes: ISO 28580 = backbone of tyre labelling systems worldwide; referenced by 15+ national regulations

regulation_name: ISO 4209 + ETRTO Standards — Tyre and Rim Dimensional Standards
regulation_ref: ISO 4209 (Truck and bus tyres dimensions and load capacity) + ETRTO Engineering Design Information
enforcement_body: ISO TC 31 + ETRTO (European Tyre and Rim Technical Organisation)
key_requirements: ISO 4209 for commercial tyres dimensional classification; ETRTO tables standard reference for passenger car tyre dimensions, load/speed indices globally; referenced by ECE R30, JATMA, TRA, ETRTO for market homologation
notes: ETRTO/ISO/JATMA/TRA form global dimensional harmonization for tyres; mandatory reference for manufacturers

regulation_name: Codex Alimentarius — Non applicable aux pneumatiques
key_requirements: Codex Alimentarius couvre normes de securite alimentaire uniquement; pneumatiques voiture ne relevent pas du cadre Codex
notes: Non applicable — produit non alimentaire

regulation_name: WADA — Non applicable aux pneumatiques voiture
key_requirements: WADA regit les substances interdites aux sportifs; pneumatiques voiture hors champ d'application
notes: Non applicable — hors champ dopage sportif

regulation_name: OECD Guidelines — Chemical substances in tyres (indirect reference via 6PPD ecotox assessment)
enforcement_body: OECD (Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development)
key_requirements: OECD ecotoxicology test guidelines (TG 203, 210 etc.) used in hazard assessment of 6PPD-quinone; referenced in ECHA restriction dossier and CA DTSC Alternatives Analysis; soft-law reference framework
notes: OECD TG indirect relevance via 6PPD hazard assessment; not a direct tyre market access requirement

key_requirements: Union authorisation or mutual recognition; PT18 (insecticides) active substance approval; product authorisation required before placing on market; efficacy & safety dossier; SPC (Summary of Product Characteristics)
notes: Covers household insecticide sprays as PT18 biocides; active substances must be approved in Annex I or under review

regulation_name: BPR Annex I — Approved Active Substances
key_requirements: Active substances used in PT18 insecticide products must be listed in Annex I or transitional list; review programme under Regulation (EU) No 1062/2014; substances not approved are banned
notes: Check each active ingredient (pyrethroids, neonicotinoids, etc.) against current Annex I approval status

key_requirements: Hazard classification of active and co-formulants; skull & crossbones / environment pictograms; signal words; precautionary statements; child-resistant closure if acute Tox Cat 1-3 or specific Tox
notes: CLP applies in parallel with BPR; label must integrate both sets of requirements

key_requirements: SVHC candidate list checks; restriction Annex XVII; substances > 1 t/y must be registered; safety data sheet required; downstream user obligations
notes: Pyrethroids and solvents in aerosol may trigger SVHC / restriction checks

key_requirements: Pressure container requirements; max fill levels; flammability labelling for aerosol format; CE-style conformity; UN2037 / UN1950 transport compatibility
notes: Spray insecticide in aerosol format must comply; propellant limits and pressure tests required

regulation_name: Code de la sante publique — biocides
key_requirements: Autorisation nationale ou reconnaissance mutuelle BPR; declaration aupres de l'autorite competente (ANSES); etiquetage en francais obligatoire; restriction d'usage en presence d'enfants
notes: ANSES est l'autorite competente nationale pour les biocides en France

regulation_ref: Avis et decisions ANSES Produits Biocides
key_requirements: Evaluation des risques toxicologiques et ecotoxicologiques; exigences additionnelles francaises pour substances CMR; restrictions d'usage particulieres pour les insecticides domestiques
notes: ANSES peut emettre restrictions additionnelles au-dela des exigences BPR

key_requirements: Product authorisation via BAuA as national CA; national authorisation for PT18; notification to BfR for toxicology assessment; German-language labelling; storage and transport requirements
notes: BAuA coordinates national authorisation under BPR; BfR advises on health risk assessment

key_requirements: Hazardous substance storage, handling and labelling obligations for professional and consumer insecticide sprays; workplace exposure limits
National authorisation or mutual recognition; Italian-language SPC and label; registration in national biocide database; specific requirements for insecticides used near food/children
notes: Italy requires translation of entire SPC into Italian; check Ministero della Salute database

enforcement_body: AEMPS — Agencia Espanola de Medicamentos y Productos Sanitarios
key_requirements: Product registration with AEMPS for PT18; Spanish-language labelling; proof of efficacy against target insect species; environmental risk assessment
notes: AEMPS administers biocides in Spain; national authorisations still coexist with BPR UAs

enforcement_body: Ctgb (College voor de toelating van gewasbeschermingsmiddelen en biociden)
key_requirements: Ctgb authorisation for PT18 biocide; detailed efficacy tests against target species; aquatic toxicity assessment; Dutch-language label; specific requirements for aerosol insecticide packaging
notes: Netherlands has strict national additional conditions; Ctgb known for rigorous dossier review

key_requirements: Separate UK market authorisation required post-Brexit; GB-specific dossier submission to HSE; UK-language label; active substance reviews under UK regime independent of EU
notes: EU BPR authorisation NOT valid in GB post-Brexit; separate GB and NI (Windsor Framework) tracks required

regulation_name: COSHH — Control of Substances Hazardous to Health
key_requirements: Hazard assessment for handling aerosol insecticides; workplace exposure limits; consumer product safety requirements

regulation_name: OPBio — Ordonnance sur les produits biocides
key_requirements: Swiss product authorisation required; mutual recognition with EU BPR possible via bilateral agreement; French/German/Italian trilingual labelling; Swiss-specific efficacy data
notes: Switzerland has autonomous biocide regime; CH-EU mutual recognition covers PT18 but requires separate Swiss filing

key_requirements: BPR applies via EEA agreement; national notification to Miljodirektoratet; Norwegian-language labelling requirements; specific restrictions on certain actives in sensitive ecosystems
notes: Norway implements EU BPR through EEA; Miljodirektoratet is national CA

key_requirements: State registration for sanitary-epidemiological products including household insecticides; Russian-language labelling; GHS-aligned hazard classification; separate from EAEU regime
notes: Russia maintains independent biocide registration system; Rospotrebnadzor issues sanitary-epidemiological conclusions

enforcement_body: Eurasian Economic Commission + National Market Surveillance
key_requirements: EAC conformity marking required; unified technical requirements for chemical products including biocides; GHS hazard communication; registration in EAEU member state before circulation
notes: Covers AM, BY, KZ, KG, RU; household insecticide spray requires EAC mark and EAEU-wide registration

enforcement_body: TUBITAK MAM + Saglik Bakanligi (Ministry of Health)
key_requirements: Pre-market authorisation for PT18 biocides; Turkish-language SPC and labelling; technical dossier submission; efficacy tests with Turkish-specific conditions; Ministry of Health approval
notes: Turkey has partially harmonised with EU BPR but maintains national authorisation system

key_requirements: State hygiene certificate required; Ukrainian-language labelling; efficacy and safety dossier; biocide registration in national register; import permit
notes: Ukraine's biocide regime is separate from EU BPR; ongoing legislative approximation post-Association Agreement

regulation_name: FIFRA — Federal Insecticide Fungicide and Rodenticide Act
enforcement_body: US EPA — Office of Pesticide Programs
key_requirements: Federal registration of insecticide product with EPA before sale; complete dossier including efficacy data, toxicology, environmental fate; EPA registration number on label; registrant responsible for re-registration and data calls
notes: All household insecticide sprays must be federally registered; EPA Reg. No. mandatory on label

key_requirements: Full technical grade active substance dossier; formulation data; laboratory efficacy tests; toxicology (acute oral/dermal/inhalation, sub-chronic, etc.); ecological risk assessment; tolerance or exemption from tolerance
notes: Parts 152 (registration requirements), 156 (labelling), 158 (data requirements) most relevant

key_requirements: Label must include EPA Reg. No., net contents, child-hazard warnings, precautionary statements, signal word, directions for use, storage/disposal; aerosol-specific precautions
notes: EPA reviews label as part of registration; non-compliant label = registration denial

key_requirements: Aerosol insecticides containing petroleum distillates >=10% must have child-resistant packaging or trigger-style cap; PPPA requirements; special packaging testing per 16 CFR 1700.20
notes: Household insecticide sprays often trigger PPPA CR packaging requirement; test with senior/child panels

key_requirements: Aerosol classification (UN1950 Aerosols flammable if applicable); proper shipping name; hazard labels; packaging; quantity limits for air transport; SDS required
notes: Aerosol insecticide = UN1950; flammability class depends on propellant; IATA/IMDG alignment

regulation_ref: California Food and Agricultural Code + 3 CCR Section 6000+
key_requirements: State pesticide registration required in addition to EPA federal registration; California-specific data requirements; restricted materials permit for certain actives; annual mill assessment; 24-hour emergency number on label
notes: California is the most stringent US state; CDPR may impose conditions beyond EPA label

key_requirements: Warning required if product contains Prop 65 listed chemicals (e.g. DEHP, certain solvents); clear and reasonable warning; website or label warning; can-specific application
notes: Many aerosol propellants and solvents are Prop 65-listed; check formulation against OEHHA list

regulation_ref: New York Environmental Conservation Law Article 33
enforcement_body: NY DEC — Division of Materials Management
key_requirements: Separate state pesticide registration required; restricted-use pesticide dealer permit for certain categories; NY-specific labelling addendum; annual reporting
notes: NY requires separate state registration even for EPA-registered products; check restricted-use classification

enforcement_body: Health Canada — PMRA (Pest Management Regulatory Agency)
key_requirements: Registration required before sale; safety, value and merit dossier; bilingual (EN/FR) labelling; PMRA re-evaluation cycle; consumer products must meet home-use classification criteria
notes: Canada has independent pesticide registration; PMRA does not accept US EPA registration as substitute

enforcement_body: Health Canada — Consumer Product Safety Directorate
key_requirements: Child-resistant packaging for household insecticide aerosols meeting flammability/toxicity thresholds; labelling requirements for hazard symbols; first aid directions; CR packaging testing per ASTM D3475

enforcement_body: COFEPRIS (Comision Federal para la Proteccion contra Riesgos Sanitarios)
key_requirements: Sanitary registration as household insecticide; Spanish-language label per NOM-232; efficacy data; toxicity classification; aerosol-specific requirements; SEMARNAT environmental assessment for active substances
notes: NOM-232-SSA1-2009 is the key labelling norm for household pesticides in Mexico

enforcement_body: ANVISA — Agencia Nacional de Vigilancia Sanitaria
key_requirements: ANVISA registration as domissanitario (household insecticide); category 3 (low toxicity) or restricted; Brazilian Portuguese labelling; efficacy proof per ANVISA protocols; manufacturing site inspection
notes: Household insecticide = domissanitario under Brazilian regulation; distinct from agricultural pesticides

key_requirements: Environmental risk assessment by IBAMA; active substance registration; environmental persistence and ecotoxicity data; IBAMA issues separate environmental registration number
notes: IBAMA, ANVISA, and MAPA form Brazil's tri-partite registration system for pesticides

key_requirements: Agricultural use component of tri-partite registration; for household-only products MAPA registration may not apply; check product classification carefully
notes: MAPA registration only required if product claims agricultural/public health use beyond household

enforcement_body: ANMAT (Administracion Nacional de Medicamentos Alimentos y Tecnologia Medica)
key_requirements: Product registration as domisanitario; efficacy and safety dossier; Argentine Spanish labelling; manufacturing licence; periodic renewal
notes: Argentina classifies household insecticides as domisanitarios regulated by ANMAT

enforcement_body: SENASA (Servicio Nacional de Sanidad y Calidad Agroalimentaria)
key_requirements: Active substance registration with SENASA for plaguicidas de uso domestico; technical grade registration separate from formulated product
notes: Argentina has dual ANMAT + SENASA registration requirement for household insecticides

enforcement_body: ISP (Instituto de Salud Publica de Chile) + SAG
key_requirements: ISP registration for household insecticides; efficacy against target species; toxicological classification; Spanish-language labelling; aerosol-specific safety data
notes: Chile distinguishes agricultural (SAG) from household (ISP) pesticide registration

enforcement_body: INVIMA (Instituto Nacional de Vigilancia de Medicamentos y Alimentos)
key_requirements: INVIMA sanitary registration as plaguicida de uso domestico; toxicity classification I-IV; Colombian Spanish labelling; efficacy studies; periodic renewal every 5 years
Harmonised classification of household pesticides within Mercosur; toxicity classes I-IV; labelling harmonisation; member states maintain national registration bodies
notes: Framework regulation; national implementations vary by member state

enforcement_body: MARA — Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Affairs
key_requirements: Pesticide registration with MARA; separate registration for active substance and formulation; efficacy and safety data per Chinese standards; Chinese-language label; GB standard compliance; annual inspection
notes: Household insecticide spray (sha chong ji) in China falls under MARA Pesticide Administration Regulation

key_requirements: If product claims disinfection in addition to insecticidal action, must comply with disinfectant standards; dual regulatory pathway may apply
notes: Insecticide-only products: MARA. Disinfectant claims: NHC pathway. Dual claims require dual registration

key_requirements: Registration with AFCD as Part I or Part II pesticide; label approval; annual registration fee; Chinese and English bilingual labelling; efficacy data required
notes: HK maintains independent pesticide registration system; China MARA registration NOT valid in HK

regulation_name: Toxic Chemical Substances Control Act + EPA TWA
regulation_ref: Toxic Chemical Substances Control Act + Measures for Registration of Pesticides
key_requirements: Registration of pesticide formulation; active substance listing; Chinese-language label; environmental risk assessment; aerosol propellant restrictions under air quality regulations

regulation_ref: Huan jing yong yao guan li fa (Environmental Agents Control Act)
key_requirements: Household insecticides classified as environmental agents; registration, manufacture, import controls; applicator licensing; disposal regulations; label must include EPA registration number
notes: Primary regulatory framework for household insecticide sprays in Taiwan

enforcement_body: Ministry of Agriculture Forestry and Fisheries (MAFF)
key_requirements: Registration of active substance and product formulation; efficacy data; residue limits; Japanese-language labelling; annual reporting; re-registration review cycle
notes: Household insecticide sprays (satsuchuzai) in Japan fall under MAFF Noyakuho registration

regulation_ref: Tokutei kagaku busshitsu no kankyo heno haishutsuryo no haaku to kanri no kaizen no sokushin ni kansuru horitsu
key_requirements: PRTR reporting obligations for certain chemical substances released to environment; threshold quantities; annual emission reporting
notes: Aerosol propellants and solvents may trigger PRTR reporting obligations

regulation_ref: Saengwal hwahakjepum mit salgyungje ui anjonggwanri e gwanhan beobeul (Act No. 16226, 2019)
enforcement_body: Ministry of Environment + Rural Development Administration
key_requirements: Registration under Biocide Safety Management Act for non-agricultural insecticides; safety assessment; efficacy data; Korean-language label; active substance approval list; QR code on label
notes: Korea's 2019 Biocide Safety Management Act created new framework for household biocides separate from agrochemicals

regulation_ref: Hwahakmuljirul deungnok mit pyeong ga deung e gwanhan beomnyul (K-REACH)
key_requirements: Registration of chemical substances >=1 t/y; GHS-compliant labelling and SDS; existing substance phase-in schedule; restricted/prohibited substance list
notes: Active substances and co-formulants in insecticide may require K-REACH registration

regulation_name: Control of Vectors and Pesticides Act + SS 539
regulation_ref: Control of Vectors and Pesticides Act (Cap 59) + SS 539:2018
key_requirements: Product registration with NEA; active substance approval; SS 539 label standard compliance; efficacy against dengue vectors (NEA priority); import licence; licence renewable annually
notes: Singapore prioritises vector control; household insecticide spray registration requires dengue vector efficacy data

key_requirements: Registration of household pesticide with DOA; technical dossier; Malay/English bilingual labelling; toxicological and efficacy data; annual licence renewal
notes: DOA Malaysia is the national pesticide authority; household insecticides require separate registration from agricultural pesticides

key_requirements: Registration as Type 3 hazardous substance; licence for import/export/manufacture/sale; Thai-language labelling; GHS-aligned classification; efficacy data
notes: Household insecticide sprays are Type 3 hazardous substances under Thai law; both DOA and FDA involvement depending on classification

enforcement_body: Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development (MARD) — Plant Protection Department
key_requirements: Registration of active substance and product; efficacy tests; Vietnamese-language labelling; environmental and health risk assessment; permitted list of active substances
notes: Vietnam's 2018 decree modernised pesticide registration; household insecticides require Product registration number

key_requirements: Registration number (nomor pendaftaran) required; Ministry of Agriculture for agricultural active substances; BPOM for consumer products safety; Indonesian-language labelling; halal certification may be required
notes: Indonesia requires both agricultural ministry clearance and BPOM consumer safety registration for household insecticides

regulation_name: FPA Fertilizer and Pesticide Authority Act
key_requirements: FPA registration certificate required; technical and label registration; efficacy data for target species; Filipino/English bilingual label; hazard classification per FPA guidelines
notes: FPA is the sole authority for pesticide registration in the Philippines including household insecticides

enforcement_body: CIB&RC — Central Insecticides Board and Registration Committee
key_requirements: Registration with CIB&RC; dossier submission including efficacy, toxicology, residue data; registration in Schedule (Part A or B); English and regional language labelling; Manufacturing licence under Insecticides Rules
notes: India's Insecticides Act covers household and public health insecticides; CIB&RC at Faridabad is the registration authority

regulation_ref: Agricultural and Veterinary Chemicals Code Act 1994 + AgVet Code
enforcement_body: APVMA (Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority)
key_requirements: Product registration with APVMA; active constituent registration; full safety and efficacy dossier; English-language label per APVMA guidelines; state/territory import permits may apply; SUSMP scheduling
notes: Household insecticide sprays are registered chemical products under AgVet Code; APVMA is Commonwealth regulator

regulation_ref: Agricultural Compounds and Veterinary Medicines Act 1997 + HSNO Act 1996
enforcement_body: ACVM Group (MPI) + Environmental Protection Authority (EPA NZ)
key_requirements: ACVM registration for household insecticide; EPA approval under HSNO Act for new hazardous substances; English-language labelling; GHS classification; import health standard; annual renewal
notes: NZ requires both ACVM registration (product efficacy/safety) and HSNO EPA approval (hazardous substance import/use)

regulation_name: ASEAN Pesticide Technical Working Group — Cooperation Framework
regulation_ref: ASEAN Agreement on Pesticides + ASEAN Common Technical Dossier (CTD)
enforcement_body: ASEAN Committee on Science and Technology — Pesticide TWG
key_requirements: Harmonisation of pesticide technical dossier format (ASEAN CTD); facilitates mutual recognition; member states retain national registration authority; data protection principles
notes: ASEAN CTD reduces duplication in dossier preparation; does not replace national registrations

regulation_ref: GSO 1942:2009 + national pesticide laws (SA: MoE/SFDA; UAE: MoCCAE; KW: PAAET; QA: MOPH; BH: NOGA; OM: MoA)
key_requirements: Registration with national authority; GSO 1942 harmonised requirements; Arabic-language labelling; efficacy data against GCC-relevant target species (mosquitoes, cockroaches); import permit
notes: GCC countries maintain national registrations; KSA (SFDA) is often the lead market; UAE, Kuwait, Qatar have own requirements

regulation_ref: Plant Protection Law 1956 (amended) + Biocide Products Regulations
enforcement_body: PPIS — Plant Protection and Inspection Services (Ministry of Agriculture)
key_requirements: Registration with PPIS; Hebrew-language label; efficacy and safety dossier; data protection; import permit; CLP-aligned hazard classification; Annex I active substance review aligned with EU
notes: Israel tends to align with EU BPR framework but maintains independent national registration

regulation_ref: Decision of Minister of Agriculture + Pesticides Act
enforcement_body: AAPC — Agricultural Pesticides Committee (Ministry of Agriculture and Land Reclamation)
key_requirements: AAPC registration for household and public health insecticides; technical file; efficacy data; Arabic-language labelling; import licence; toxicological classification
notes: Egypt's AAPC also covers household insecticides used for public health purposes
